title: Magnetron / Inverter Circuit Fault
shortDescription: The inverter circuit driving the magnetron has detected a critical fault.
fullDescription: Error code H97 on Panasonic microwave ovens indicates a fault in the inverter circuit that powers the magnetron. Panasonic microwaves use proprietary inverter technology rather than a traditional transformer, and H97 signals a failure in this system. The microwave will not heat food until repaired.

causes.cause: Inverter board component failure (transistors or capacitors)
Magnetron tube failure causing inverter overload
Power surge damage to inverter circuitry
Inverter board solder joint crack from thermal cycling

symptoms: Microwave runs but does not heat food
Microwave shuts off immediately when cook button is pressed
Loud buzzing or humming from the inverter area
Burning electronic smell from inside the unit

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the microwave
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the microwave for 5 minutes, then plug it back in. This resets the inverter board and may clear a transient fault.

fixSteps.title: Check the power supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the outlet voltage is stable and within specification. Plug the microwave into a different outlet on a separate circuit to rule out power issues.

fixSteps.title: Check the door switches
fixSteps.instruction: WARNING: Microwaves contain high-voltage components. Ensure the microwave is unplugged before any inspection. Verify all door interlock switches click properly when the door is closed. A faulty switch can cause inverter errors.

fixSteps.title: Professional inverter board replacement
fixSteps.instruction: H97 typically requires replacement of the inverter board assembly. This must be done by a qualified technician due to the high-voltage capacitor and magnetron connections.

whenToCallPro: Always call a professional for H97. Microwave inverter circuits operate at lethal voltages and the high-voltage capacitor can retain charge even when unplugged.
videoSearchQuery: Panasonic microwave H97 error code inverter fix

faq.a: If the microwave is a higher-end Panasonic inverter model and less than 5 years old, repair is often cost-effective at 200-450 professional cost. For basic models, replacement may be more practical.

faq.q: What makes Panasonic inverter microwaves special?
faq.a: Panasonic inverter microwaves deliver consistent power levels unlike conventional microwaves that cycle on and off. This provides more even cooking results.

faq.q: Can I fix the inverter board myself?
faq.a: Strongly discouraged. The inverter board operates at voltages exceeding 4,000 volts and the high-voltage capacitor can store a lethal charge. Professional repair is essential.

title: Magnetron Overheat / Thermal Cutoff
shortDescription: The magnetron has overheated and the thermal cutoff has activated.
fullDescription: Error code H98 indicates the magnetron thermal cutoff (thermostat) has tripped due to excessive temperature. The microwave shuts down to protect the magnetron from permanent damage. This is commonly caused by blocked ventilation, a failed cooling fan, or extended high-power operation.

causes.cause: Cooling fan motor failure or obstruction
Ventilation openings blocked by nearby objects
Grease and debris clogging internal airflow paths
Magnetron thermal fuse blown

symptoms: Excessive heat from the ventilation area
Cooling fan runs louder than normal or not at all
Error appears after extended cooking sessions

fixSteps.title: Let the microwave cool down
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the microwave and allow it to cool for at least 30 minutes. The thermal cutoff may reset once the temperature drops to a safe level.

fixSteps.title: Clear ventilation openings
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure all ventilation openings on the microwave are clear. Remove any items placed on top of, behind, or beside the unit that may block airflow. Over-the-range models need clear air intake at the bottom.

fixSteps.title: Clean the grease filter
fixSteps.instruction: For over-the-range models, remove and clean the grease filter. A clogged filter restricts airflow and contributes to overheating. Soak in hot soapy water and scrub.

fixSteps.title: Check the cooling fan
fixSteps.instruction: WARNING: Unplug and wait at least 5 minutes before inspection. Listen for the cooling fan during operation. If the fan is not running, the fan motor may need replacement. A qualified technician should diagnose and replace the fan.

whenToCallPro: If the cooling fan or thermal fuse needs replacement, as accessing these components requires disassembling the microwave, which involves high-voltage hazards.
videoSearchQuery: Panasonic microwave H98 error code overheat thermal fix

faq.q: Is it safe to continue using the microwave after H98 clears?
faq.a: If H98 was caused by blocked ventilation, clearing the blockage and allowing proper cooling should prevent recurrence. If it recurs, the cooling fan or thermal fuse needs attention.

faq.q: How long can I run the microwave continuously?
faq.a: Most Panasonic microwaves can run continuously for up to 30 minutes on high power with adequate ventilation. Extended operation beyond this may trigger H98.

faq.q: Can I replace the thermal fuse myself?
faq.a: This is not recommended for microwaves. The high-voltage capacitor can store a lethal charge even when unplugged. Always have a qualified technician perform internal microwave repairs.

title: Control Board General Fault
shortDescription: The microwave control board has detected an unspecified internal fault.
fullDescription: Error code H99 is a general fault code indicating the main control board has detected an error it cannot categorize. This can be caused by power surges, component failure, or firmware issues. The microwave may be completely non-functional or exhibit erratic behavior.

causes.cause: Power surge or voltage spike damage
Control board microprocessor failure
Multiple component failures
Door interlock switch malfunction confusing the control logic

symptoms: Random button presses or display changes
Cannot clear the error by normal means

fixSteps.title: Hard reset the microwave
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes. Press the Start button several times while unplugged to discharge internal capacitors safely. Plug back in and check if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Check door switches
fixSteps.instruction: Open and close the door firmly several times. Verify the door latches securely and all switches click audibly. A misaligned door or faulty switch can cause H99.

fixSteps.title: Test on a different circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Plug the microwave into a different outlet on a separate circuit breaker. Unstable power or a shared circuit with a noisy appliance can cause control board errors.

fixSteps.title: Professional control board replacement
fixSteps.instruction: If H99 persists, the main control board needs replacement. This requires professional service due to high-voltage components.

whenToCallPro: If a hard reset does not clear H99, professional diagnosis and likely board replacement is needed.
videoSearchQuery: Panasonic microwave H99 error code control board fix

faq.q: Should I repair or replace my microwave with H99?
faq.a: For premium Panasonic inverter models under 5 years old, repair (150-400) is typically worthwhile. For basic models or older units, a new microwave may be more economical.
A simple button press will not clear a true H99 hardware error. A full power disconnection for 10 minutes is required to reset the control board.

faq.q: Is H99 caused by a lightning strike?
faq.a: Power surges from lightning or utility switching are a common cause of H99. Using a surge protector can prevent this type of damage.

title: Humidity Sensor Error
shortDescription: The humidity sensor used for auto-cook programs is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error code F04 indicates the humidity sensor (steam sensor) that detects cooking progress for automatic cooking programs has failed. This sensor measures steam emitted from food to determine when cooking is complete. Without it, auto-cook, reheat, and sensor-based programs will not function correctly.

causes.cause: Humidity sensor element contaminated with grease
Humidity sensor component failure
Sensor wiring disconnected or damaged
Control board sensor input circuit failure

symptoms: Sensor reheat over or under-cooks food
Manual timed cooking still works normally

fixSteps.title: Clean the microwave interior
fixSteps.instruction: Thoroughly clean the inside of the microwave, paying special attention to the ceiling where the humidity sensor is located. Use a damp cloth with mild detergent to remove grease and food splatters.

fixSteps.title: Clean the sensor directly
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the humidity sensor, usually a small disc or rectangular element on the ceiling of the microwave cavity. Gently clean it with a soft, damp cloth. Do not use abrasive cleaners.

fixSteps.title: Reset the microwave
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes and restart. The sensor may need recalibration which happens automatically on power-up.

fixSteps.title: Replace the humidity sensor
fixSteps.instruction: WARNING: High-voltage hazard. If cleaning does not resolve F04, the sensor needs replacement by a qualified technician. The sensor is typically accessible from the top of the cavity behind the outer cover.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning does not resolve the issue and the sensor needs physical replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Panasonic microwave F04 error code humidity sensor fix

faq.q: Can I still use manual cooking with F04?
faq.a: Yes. Manual timed cooking and power level controls work normally. Only the automatic sensor-based cooking programs are affected by F04.

faq.q: Why does grease affect the humidity sensor?
faq.a: The humidity sensor detects moisture in the air. A grease coating prevents accurate moisture detection, causing incorrect readings. Regular interior cleaning prevents this.

faq.q: How does the sensor cook feature work?
faq.a: The sensor detects steam released by heating food. When steam reaches a certain level, the microwave knows the food has reached the target temperature and adjusts cooking time accordingly.

title: Temperature Sensor / Thermistor Error
shortDescription: The internal temperature sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: Error code F06 indicates the cavity temperature sensor (thermistor) that monitors internal temperature for convection baking and combination cooking modes has failed. This sensor ensures the oven cavity reaches and maintains the correct temperature for baking and grilling functions.

causes.cause: Thermistor wiring connector loose or corroded
Thermistor element failed (open or short)
Temperature extremes damaged the sensor
Control board temperature input circuit fault

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the microwave
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes and restart. A temporary sensor reading outside normal range may reset.

fixSteps.title: Let the oven cool completely
fixSteps.instruction: If F06 appeared after using the convection or grill feature, allow the oven to cool completely to room temperature before restarting. The sensor may have temporarily over-ranged.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: WARNING: High-voltage hazard. Unplug and wait at least 5 minutes. Access the thermistor, usually located on the oven cavity wall. Check that the wiring connector is securely attached.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor and test resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature (25C), expect approximately 10-100 kohms depending on model. If out of range, replace the thermistor.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable accessing the microwave interior. The high-voltage capacitor makes all internal microwave work potentially dangerous.
videoSearchQuery: Panasonic microwave oven F06 error code temperature sensor fix

faq.a: No. F06 only affects the convection, grill, and combination cooking modes that require temperature monitoring. Standard microwave heating works normally.

faq.q: Can I use the microwave mode while F06 is showing?
faq.a: Most Panasonic combination ovens will allow microwave-only operation with F06 active, but convection modes will be disabled.
No, the thermistor is one of the least expensive parts at 10-30. However, professional installation is recommended due to microwave high-voltage hazards.
